summary: Matched two tydisplay functions, renamed/split two formerly unknown un_2FC9 translation units into interface modules ifnametag and ifhazard, moved their declarations into new headers, updated build/split metadata, and cleaned up includes/prototypes around the affected systems. The rationale given in the PR body was usage-based: un_2FC91 became ifnametag based on existing function usage/names, and un_2FC92 became ifhazard because it is used on Onett and Big Blue and likely relates to those stages' hazard indicator.
what_changed: Renamed split entry melee/un/un_2FC91.c to melee/if/ifnametag.c in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
Renamed split entry melee/un/un_2FC92.c to melee/if/ifhazard.c in config/GALE01/splits.txt.

Moved build configuration for ifnametag.c and ifhazard.c from the Unknown library group to the interface library group in configure.py; ifhazard.c is marked Matching while ifnametag.c remains NonMatching.
Renamed symbols un_8031C2CC and un_8031C2EC to tyDisplay_8031C2CC and tyDisplay_8031C2EC in config/GALE01/symbols.txt and src/melee/ty/tydisplay.h.
Implemented tyDisplay_8031C2CC as a thin wrapper around un_803124BC.
Implemented tyDisplay_8031C2EC with a conditional call to un_80304B0C(5), a random branch via HSD_Randi(2), and calls to un_80305058 with either 65.0f or 60.0f parameters.
Updated src/melee/gr/ground.c to call tyDisplay_8031C2CC and tyDisplay_8031C2EC instead of the previous un_8031C2CC and un_8031C2EC names.
Created src/melee/if/ifnametag.h and moved nametag-related declarations out of src/melee/un/un_2FC9.h.
Created src/melee/if/ifhazard.h and moved hazard-related declarations out of src/melee/un/un_2FC9.h.
Moved NAMETAG_DISABLED from src/melee/un/forward.h into src/melee/if/ifnametag.h.
Updated includes in gm_16AE.c, gronett.c, ifall.c, ifnametag.c, and ifhazard.c to use the new if/ifnametag.h and if/ifhazard.h headers.
Tightened the prototype for un_80305058 in src/melee/ty/toy.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 un_80305058(s32, s32, s32, f32).
Performed include cleanup in renamed files, removing many broad or stale includes from the former un_2FC91.c and un_2FC92.c paths.
smart_moves: Used caller context and existing named functions to justify moving unknown interface-like files out of melee/un and into melee/if.
Kept partially understood function names address-based where semantics were still uncertain, while giving the translation units and headers stronger subsystem names.
Created narrow subsystem headers instead of leaving unrelated nametag and hazard prototypes in the catch-all un_2FC9.h.
Updated split metadata, build object grouping, symbol names, headers, and call sites together, avoiding a rename that only changed source paths.
Matched tiny tydisplay functions by expressing them with direct wrapper/control-flow logic and a more precise dependency prototype for un_80305058.
Moved NAMETAG_DISABLED into the nametag header, placing the constant nearer to the subsystem it describes.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown file's usage strongly maps to an existing subsystem, rename the file and module path even if many internal functions still need address-based names.
For module migrations, update config/GALE01/splits.txt and configure.py at the same time as the source rename so build layout and decomp splits remain aligned.
New subsystem headers can reduce pressure on large unknown headers and make future type/prototype refinement easier.
A small matched function may require improving prototypes in dependent headers; here un_80305058 needed concrete s32 and f32 parameter/return types for tydisplay matching.
Call-site renames such as un_8031C2CC to tyDisplay_8031C2CC should be reflected in symbols.txt, headers, and users to preserve searchable consistency.
Usage on specific stages can be useful naming evidence, but the PR itself phrases the hazard interpretation as likely rather than fully proven.
naming_conventions: Use subsystem-prefixed names for identified functions, e.g. tyDisplay_8031C2CC and tyDisplay_8031C2EC, while retaining the address suffix for unresolved semantics.
Move files from un/un_*.c to subsystem paths like if/ifnametag.c and if/ifhazard.c when evidence supports an interface role.
Header guards follow uppercase underscore subsystem names, shown by _IFNAMETAG_H_ and _IFHAZARD_H_.
Constants tied to a subsystem should live with that subsystem's header, as with NAMETAG_DISABLED in ifnametag.h.
Known semantic function names can coexist with address-based names in the same header, as shown by NameTag_Create alongside un_802FD28C-style declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched tyDisplay_8031C2CC as a void wrapper that only calls un_803124BC.
Matched tyDisplay_8031C2EC using nested conditionals: check un_80304B0C(5), branch on HSD_Randi(2) == 0, otherwise fall through to a default un_80305058 call.
Used exact float constants 65.0f and 60.0f in tyDisplay_8031C2EC, paired with concrete integer arguments to un_80305058.
Changed un_80305058's declaration from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 un_80305058(s32, s32, s32, f32), likely enabling cleaner C for the matched tydisplay call site.
Preserved PAD_STACK(8) in Ground_801C5878 while only renaming the tydisplay calls, indicating the function still needed stack-shaping despite call-site cleanup.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.

The PR body states the author's reasoning: match some tydisplay functions; rename un_2FC91 to ifnametag based on usage and previously named functions; rename un_2FC92 to ifhazard because it is used on Onett and Big Blue and likely ties to the hazard indicator.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename functions in ifnametag.h or ifhazard.h from address-based names to semantic names.
Search for additional uses of ifhazard on Onett and Big Blue to confirm the hazard-indicator interpretation.
Inspect later changes to tyDisplay_8031C2EC and un_80305058 for improved trophy/display naming.
Check whether ifnametag.c later becomes Matching and what remaining blockers were resolved.
Search for remaining declarations in un_2FC9.h that can be split into subsystem headers.
